title: Roblox Stock Crashes 24% Pre-Market: Analysts Warn of Guidance Cut
text: Roblox stock crashed 24% in pre-market trading following the company's earnings report and guidance revision for 2026. The sharp selloff reflected market disappointment with management's decision to lower full-year bookings and revenue growth expectations. Despite beating Q1 estimates on revenue and bookings, the company's 2026 guidance cut dominated investor sentiment. Full-year bookings guidance was revised from $8.28-$8.55 billion down to $7.33-$7.6 billion, representing an 8-12% growth rate versus the prior 22-26% guidance. Management attributed the revision to the impact of age-verification requirements and safety measures on user acquisition. Analysts acknowledged the company's commitment to child safety but questioned whether the magnitude of the guidance cut was proportionate. The pre-market crash illustrated market skepticism about near-term growth trajectory. Key concerns included whether DAU growth would stabilize in subsequent quarters as management expected. Sell-side analysts reviewed their price targets and earnings models in response to the guidance revision.
